name: grafana-cloud
description: Search Grafana Cloud dashboards, folders, annotations, and data sources

envVars.name: GRAFANA_CLOUD_TOKEN
envVars.description: Grafana service account token for your Grafana Cloud stack

tools.name: Search Dashboards
tools.description: Search dashboards and folders in the Grafana stack

stack.description: Grafana Cloud stack slug — the subdomain of your Grafana URL (e.g. mycompany for mycompany.grafana.net)

query.description: Search term matched against dashboard and folder titles

tag.description: Only return results with this tag

tools.name: Get Dashboard
tools.description: Get a dashboard's full JSON model by UID

tools.name: List Folders
tools.description: List dashboard folders in the Grafana stack

tools.name: List Annotations
tools.description: List annotations and alert events in a time range

from.description: Start of the window as epoch milliseconds

to.description: End of the window as epoch milliseconds

type.description: Filter by type: alert or annotation

tags.description: Filter by annotation tag, e.g. deploy

tools.name: Create Annotation
tools.description: Create an annotation (e.g. mark a deploy) on a dashboard or globally

dashboardUID.description: Dashboard UID to attach the annotation to (omit for a global annotation)

time.description: Annotation time as epoch milliseconds (defaults to now)

timeEnd.description: End time as epoch milliseconds, for region annotations

tools.name: List Data Sources
tools.description: List data sources configured in the Grafana stack

prompts.title: Find a dashboard
prompts.prompt: Search my Grafana Cloud stack for dashboards about latency and list their titles and folders.

prompts.title: Recent annotations
prompts.prompt: List the annotations in my Grafana stack from the last 24 hours and summarize any alert events.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Grafana Cloud API Setup

steps.title: Find your stack slug
steps.description: Your Grafana Cloud stack URL looks like https://<stack>.grafana.net — the subdomain is the 'stack' parameter every tool requires. Free Grafana Cloud accounts include one stack.

steps.title: Create a service account
steps.description: In your stack, go to Administration → Users and access → Service accounts, and create a service account with the Viewer role (Editor if you want to create annotations).

steps.title: Generate a token
steps.description: Add a token to the service account and copy it to your .env as GRAFANA_CLOUD_TOKEN.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run Search Dashboards with your stack slug.

notes: Tokens are scoped to a single stack; pass that stack's slug as the 'stack' parameter on every call
Service account tokens are sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'
These tools call the Grafana instance HTTP API (dashboards, folders, annotations, data sources) — not the Cloud portal API or hosted Loki/Prometheus endpoints
